---
# Arrest-warrant render. Single-page output modeled on the federal
# AO 442 form layout (court header, plaintiff-v-defendant caption,
# YOU ARE COMMANDED command, charging-document checkboxes, narrative
# offense description, signature/date footer, and a Return box).
# Adapted to the fictional jurisdiction.
#
# Render with:  quarto render arrest-warrant-only.qmd

format: warrant-vignette-typst
document-types:
  - arrest-warrant

# ---- Jurisdiction (fixed) ----
state: Westbridge
county: Hartwell County
city: Northfield
court-name: Hartwell County District Court
judicial-district: 4th Judicial District

# ---- Personnel (fixed) ----
judge-name: Hon. R. Patterson
judge-title-full: County Judge — 4th Judicial District
detective-name: Detective J. Hayes

# ---- Per-case (variable) ----
case-number: CR24-087
filed-date: AUG 12 2024

# ---- Arrest-warrant specific ----
# defendant-name defaults to suspect-names[0] if not set, but
# you can override here for clarity.
defendant-name: "[Suspect A]"

# Which charging document was filed. Valid values:
#   indictment | superseding-indictment
#   information | superseding-information
#   complaint
#   probation-violation-petition | supervised-release-violation-petition
#   violation-notice | order-of-the-court
charging-document-type: complaint

# Multi-paragraph narrative explanation of the charges. Each entry
# becomes its own indented paragraph in the "This offense is briefly
# described as follows:" section. Edit per condition.
charges-description:
  - "On 04-26-24, the defendant did unlawfully procure and administer medications for the purpose of inducing the termination of a pregnancy at approximately 23 weeks' gestation, in violation of [Westbridge statute reference]."
  - "On or about 04-29-24, the defendant did transport and conceal the remains of the unborn child by burial on private property north of Northfield, in violation of [Westbridge statute reference]."
  - "Investigation has further revealed that the defendant communicated electronically with a co-defendant on 04-20-24 concerning the procurement and use of the medications referenced in the above counts, and made internet searches consistent with planning the alleged offenses."
  - "Probable cause is supported by medical records, electronic communications obtained pursuant to lawful search, and witness statements detailed in the affidavit filed contemporaneously with this application."

suspect-names:
  - "[Suspect A]"
  - "[Suspect B]"
---
